functions.name: oidc_provider.tests.app.utils.create_fake_user
documentation:
Create a test user.

Return a User object.

functions.name: oidc_provider.tests.app.utils.create_fake_client
documentation:
Create a test client, response_type argument MUST be:
'code', 'id_token' or 'id_token token'.

Return a Client object.

functions.name: oidc_provider.tests.app.utils.is_code_valid
documentation: Check if the code inside the url is valid. Supporting both query string and fragment.

functions.name: oidc_provider.tests.app.utils.userinfo
documentation: Fake function for setting OIDC_USERINFO.

functions.name: oidc_provider.tests.app.utils.fake_sub_generator
documentation: Fake function for setting OIDC_IDTOKEN_SUB_GENERATOR.

functions.name: oidc_provider.tests.app.utils.fake_idtoken_processing_hook
documentation: Fake function for inserting some keys into token. Testing OIDC_IDTOKEN_PROCESSING_HOOK.

functions.name: oidc_provider.tests.app.utils.fake_idtoken_processing_hook2
documentation:
Fake function for inserting some keys into token.
Testing OIDC_IDTOKEN_PROCESSING_HOOK - tuple or list as param

documentation: Fake function for checking scope is passed to processing hook.

functions.name: oidc_provider.views.userinfo
documentation:
Create a dictionary with all the requested claims about the End-User.
See: http://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#UserInfoResponse

Return a dictionary.

methods.name: oidc_provider.views.ProviderInfoView._build_cache_key
documentation: Cache key will be a combination of site URL and types supported by the provider.

methods.name: oidc_provider.lib.claims.ScopeClaims.create_response_dic
documentation:
Generate the dic that will be jsonify. Checking scopes given vs
registered.

Returns a dic.

methods.name: oidc_provider.lib.claims.ScopeClaims._scopes_registered
documentation:
Return a list that contains all the scopes registered
in the class.

methods.name: oidc_provider.lib.claims.ScopeClaims._clean_dic
documentation: Clean recursively all empty or None values inside a dict.

classes.name: oidc_provider.lib.claims.StandardScopeClaims
documentation:
Based on OpenID Standard Claims.
See: http://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#StandardClaims

classes.name: oidc_provider.lib.errors.UserAuthError
documentation:
Specific to the Resource Owner Password Credentials flow when
the Resource Owners credentials are not valid.

classes.name: oidc_provider.lib.errors.TokenIntrospectionError
documentation:
Specific to the introspection endpoint. This error will be converted
to an "active: false" response, as per the spec.
See https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc7662

classes.name: oidc_provider.lib.errors.TokenError
documentation:
OAuth2 token endpoint errors.
https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6749#section-5.2

classes.name: oidc_provider.lib.errors.BearerTokenError
documentation:
OAuth2 errors.
https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6750#section-3.1

methods.name: oidc_provider.lib.endpoints.introspection.TokenIntrospectionEndpoint.response
documentation: Create and return a response object.

functions.name: oidc_provider.lib.utils.common.redirect
documentation: Custom Response object for redirecting to a Non-HTTP url scheme.

functions.name: oidc_provider.lib.utils.common.get_site_url
documentation:
Construct the site url.

Orders to decide site url:
    1. valid `site_url` parameter
    2. valid `SITE_URL` in settings
    3. construct from `request` object

functions.name: oidc_provider.lib.utils.common.get_issuer
documentation:
Construct the issuer full url. Basically is the site url with some path
appended.

functions.name: oidc_provider.lib.utils.common.default_userinfo
documentation:
Default function for setting OIDC_USERINFO.
`claims` is a dict that contains all the OIDC standard claims.

functions.name: oidc_provider.lib.utils.common.default_sub_generator
documentation: Default function for setting OIDC_IDTOKEN_SUB_GENERATOR.

functions.name: oidc_provider.lib.utils.common.default_after_userlogin_hook
documentation: Default function for setting OIDC_AFTER_USERLOGIN_HOOK.

functions.name: oidc_provider.lib.utils.common.default_after_end_session_hook
documentation:
Default function for setting OIDC_AFTER_END_SESSION_HOOK.

```
param request

Django request object

type request

django.http.HttpRequest

param id_token

token passed by  url query param.
Do NOT trust this param or validate token

type id_token

str

param post_logout_redirect_uri

redirect url from url query param.
Do NOT trust this param

type post_logout_redirect_uri

str

param state

state param from url query params

type state

str

param client

If id_token has  param and associated Client exists,
this is an instance of it - do NOT trust this param

type client

oidc_provider.models.Client

param next_page

calculated next_page redirection target

type next_page

str

return

```

functions.name: oidc_provider.lib.utils.common.default_idtoken_processing_hook
documentation:
Hook to perform some additional actions to `id_token` dictionary just before serialization.

```
param id_token

dictionary contains values that going to be serialized into 

type id_token

dict

param user

user for whom id_token is generated

type user

User

param token

the Token object created for the authentication request

type token

oidc_provider.models.Token

param request

the request initiating this ID token processing

type request

django.http.HttpRequest

return

custom modified dictionary of values for 

rtype

dict
```

functions.name: oidc_provider.lib.utils.common.default_introspection_processing_hook
documentation:
Hook to customise the returned data from the token introspection endpoint
\:param introspection_response:
\:param client:
\:param id_token:
\:return:

functions.name: oidc_provider.lib.utils.common.get_browser_state_or_default
documentation: Determine value to use as session state.

functions.name: oidc_provider.lib.utils.common.cors_allow_any
documentation:
Add headers to permit CORS requests from any origin, with or without credentials,
with any headers.

methods.name: oidc_provider.lib.endpoints.authorize.AuthorizeEndpoint._extract_params
documentation:
Get all the params used by the Authorization Code Flow
(and also for the Implicit and Hybrid).

See: http://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#AuthRequest

methods.name: oidc_provider.lib.endpoints.authorize.AuthorizeEndpoint.set_client_user_consent
documentation:
Save the user consent given to a specific client.

Return None.

methods.name: oidc_provider.lib.endpoints.authorize.AuthorizeEndpoint.client_has_user_consent
documentation:
Check if already exists user consent for some client.

Return bool.

methods.name: oidc_provider.lib.endpoints.authorize.AuthorizeEndpoint.get_scopes_information
documentation: Return a list with the description of all the scopes requested.

methods.name: oidc_provider.lib.endpoints.token.TokenEndpoint.validate_requested_scopes
documentation: Handling validation of requested scope for grant_type=[password|client_credentials]

methods.name: oidc_provider.lib.endpoints.token.TokenEndpoint.response
documentation: Create and return a response object.

functions.name: oidc_provider.lib.utils.authorize.strip_prompt_login
documentation: Strips 'login' from the 'prompt' query parameter.

functions.name: oidc_provider.lib.utils.oauth2.extract_access_token
documentation:
Get the access token using Authorization Request Header Field method.
Or try getting via GET.
See: http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6750#section-2.1

Return a string.

functions.name: oidc_provider.lib.utils.oauth2.extract_client_auth
documentation:
Get client credentials using HTTP Basic Authentication method.
Or try getting parameters via POST.
See: http://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6750#section-2.1

Return a tuple `(client_id, client_secret)`.

functions.name: oidc_provider.lib.utils.oauth2.protected_resource_view
documentation:
View decorator. The client accesses protected resources by presenting the
access token to the resource server.
https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6749#section-7

functions.name: oidc_provider.lib.utils.token.create_id_token
documentation:
Creates the id_token dictionary.
See: http://openid.net/specs/openid-connect-core-1_0.html#IDToken
Return a dic.

functions.name: oidc_provider.lib.utils.token.encode_id_token
documentation:
Represent the ID Token as a JSON Web Token (JWT).
Return a hash.

functions.name: oidc_provider.lib.utils.token.decode_id_token
documentation:
Represent the ID Token as a JSON Web Token (JWT).
Return a hash.

functions.name: oidc_provider.lib.utils.token.client_id_from_id_token
documentation:
Extracts the client id from a JSON Web Token (JWT).
Returns a string or None.

functions.name: oidc_provider.lib.utils.token.create_token
documentation:
Create and populate a Token object.
Return a Token object.

functions.name: oidc_provider.lib.utils.token.create_code
documentation:
Create and populate a Code object.
Return a Code object.

functions.name: oidc_provider.lib.utils.token.get_client_alg_keys
documentation:
Takes a client and returns the set of keys associated with it.
Returns a list of keys.

modules.name: oidc_provider.signals
documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

functions.name: oidc_provider.settings.import_from_str
documentation: Attempt to import a class from a string representation.

functions.name: oidc_provider.settings.get
documentation: Helper function to use inside the package.

methods.name: oidc_provider.settings.DefaultSettings.OIDC_LOGIN_URL
documentation: OPTIONAL. Used to log the user in. By default Django's LOGIN_URL will be used.

methods.name: oidc_provider.settings.DefaultSettings.SITE_URL
documentation: OPTIONAL. The OP server url.

methods.name: oidc_provider.settings.DefaultSettings.OIDC_AFTER_USERLOGIN_HOOK
documentation:
OPTIONAL.  Provide a way to plug into the process after
the user has logged in, typically to perform some business logic.

methods.name: oidc_provider.settings.DefaultSettings.OIDC_AFTER_END_SESSION_HOOK
documentation:
OPTIONAL.  Provide a way to plug into the end session process just before calling
 Django's logout function, typically to perform some business logic.

methods.name: oidc_provider.settings.DefaultSettings.OIDC_CODE_EXPIRE
documentation: OPTIONAL. Code expiration time expressed in seconds.

methods.name: oidc_provider.settings.DefaultSettings.OIDC_DISCOVERY_CACHE_ENABLE
documentation: OPTIONAL. Enable caching the response on the discovery endpoint.

methods.name: oidc_provider.settings.DefaultSettings.OIDC_DISCOVERY_CACHE_EXPIRE
documentation: OPTIONAL. Discovery endpoint cache expiration time expressed in seconds.

methods.name: oidc_provider.settings.DefaultSettings.OIDC_EXTRA_SCOPE_CLAIMS
documentation:
OPTIONAL. A string with the location of your class.
Used to add extra scopes specific for your app.

methods.name: oidc_provider.settings.DefaultSettings.OIDC_IDTOKEN_EXPIRE
documentation: OPTIONAL. Id token expiration time expressed in seconds.

methods.name: oidc_provider.settings.DefaultSettings.OIDC_IDTOKEN_SUB_GENERATOR
documentation:
OPTIONAL. Subject Identifier. A locally unique and never
reassigned identifier within the Issuer for the End-User,
which is intended to be consumed by the Client.

methods.name: oidc_provider.settings.DefaultSettings.OIDC_IDTOKEN_INCLUDE_CLAIMS
documentation: OPTIONAL. If enabled, id_token will include standard claims of the user.

methods.name: oidc_provider.settings.DefaultSettings.OIDC_SESSION_MANAGEMENT_ENABLE
documentation: OPTIONAL. If enabled, the Server will support Session Management 1.0 specification.

methods.name: oidc_provider.settings.DefaultSettings.OIDC_UNAUTHENTICATED_SESSION_MANAGEMENT_KEY
documentation: OPTIONAL. Supply a fixed string to use as browser-state key for unauthenticated clients.

methods.name: oidc_provider.settings.DefaultSettings.OIDC_SKIP_CONSENT_EXPIRE
documentation: OPTIONAL. User consent expiration after been granted.

methods.name: oidc_provider.settings.DefaultSettings.OIDC_TOKEN_EXPIRE
documentation:
OPTIONAL. Token object expiration after been created.
Expressed in seconds.

methods.name: oidc_provider.settings.DefaultSettings.OIDC_USERINFO
documentation:
OPTIONAL. A string with the location of your function.
Used to populate standard claims with your user information.

methods.name: oidc_provider.settings.DefaultSettings.OIDC_IDTOKEN_PROCESSING_HOOK
documentation:
OPTIONAL. A string with the location of your hook.
Used to add extra dictionary values specific for your app into id_token.

methods.name: oidc_provider.settings.DefaultSettings.OIDC_INTROSPECTION_PROCESSING_HOOK
documentation:
OPTIONAL. A string with the location of your function.
Used to update the response for a valid introspection token request.

methods.name: oidc_provider.settings.DefaultSettings.OIDC_INTROSPECTION_VALIDATE_AUDIENCE_SCOPE
documentation:
OPTIONAL: A boolean to specify whether or not to verify that the introspection
resource has the requesting client id as one of its scopes.

methods.name: oidc_provider.settings.DefaultSettings.OIDC_GRANT_TYPE_PASSWORD_ENABLE
documentation:
OPTIONAL. A boolean to set whether to allow the Resource Owner Password
Credentials Grant. https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6749#section-4.3

From the specification:
    Since this access token request utilizes the resource owner's
    password, the authorization server MUST protect the endpoint
    against brute force attacks (e.g., using rate-limitation or
    generating alerts).

How you do this, is up to you.

methods.name: oidc_provider.settings.DefaultSettings.OIDC_INTROSPECTION_RESPONSE_SCOPE_ENABLE
documentation: OPTIONAL: A boolean to specify whether or not to include scope in introspection response.

classes.name: oidc_provider.middleware.SessionManagementMiddleware
documentation:
Maintain a `op_browser_state` cookie along with the `sessionid` cookie that
represents the End-User's login state at the OP. If the user is not logged
in then use the value of settings.OIDC_UNAUTHENTICATED_SESSION_MANAGEMENT_KEY.
